{"society_power": 9.0, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Third Reform Act (1884) expands electorate; balanced Red Queen growth", "reasoning": "The Third Reform Act, New Unionism, and press expansion deepened an already dense civil society, while professionalized bureaucracy and coercive Irish legislation reinforced a strong shackled state\u2014but both were already near the top of the rubric, so these mutually reinforcing gains keep Britain firmly within the corridor without a decisive shift in either near-ceiling value."}
